task_completion.description: Open the 'T1 Head' sample image in ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ application is open with the 'T1 Head' image displayed. The image window title should initially show 't1-head' and dimensions like '256x256x129; 16-bit'.
The ImageJ window title bar updates to show '8-bit' instead of '16-bit', or the 'Image > Type > 8-bit' menu option is selected.

task_completion.description: Scale the spatial dimensions to 75% of the original size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ window title bar updates to show dimensions around '192x192' instead of '256x256', or the 'Image > Scale' dialog is visible with X and Y scale set to 0.75 (or 75%).

task_completion.description: Subsample the stack to keep only every 3rd slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ window title bar updates to show a total slice count of around 43 (e.g., '1/43' or '43 slices'), or a stack manipulation dialog (like Slice Keeper or Make Substack) is used with an increment/step of 3.

task_completion.description: Save the processed stack as a TIFF file to ~/ImageJ_Data/processed/mri_preview.tif.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box is open with the filename 'mri_preview.tif' and the directory path pointing to 'ImageJ_Data/processed/', or a terminal/file explorer confirms the file exists at the specified path.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ GUI to process the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show ImageJ menus, dialogs, and image windows being interacted with, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the image manipulation steps in ImageJ.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image window title bar dynamically updates its metadata (bit depth, dimensions, slice count) as steps are performed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original 'T1 Head' sample image has dimensions 256x256, 129 slices, and is 16-bit. After correctly applying the task requirements, the image should be 8-bit, scaled to 192x192 (75% of 256), and have 43 slices (every 3rd slice of 129). The final file must be saved to ~/ImageJ_Data/processed/mri_preview.tif.
